sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for NES port

special_mechanics.name: Biplane Flight
special_mechanics.description: Player controls a biplane through vertical scrolling levels. Background continuously scrolls until runway is reached. No documented jump/gravity mechanics applicable as this is a flying shmup.

physics_values.notes: Shmup genre - traditional platformer physics not applicable

special_mechanics.name: Bomb Weapon System
special_mechanics.description: Powerful bomb weapon capable of obliterating enemies within blast radius. Player starts with 3 bombs per stage, can collect 'B' icons from destroyed enemies to increase bomb stock.

special_mechanics.name: Standard Shot
special_mechanics.description: Primary weapon that travels maximum distance of screen height

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Empirical/Qualitative. Sky Shark (NES) is a vertically scrolling shoot 'em up developed by Software Creations in 1989. No quantitative movement, gravity, or jump physics values are documented in available sources. This is a shmup title where traditional platformer physics (walking speed, jumping, gravity) do not apply. Game focuses on biplane movement through pre-scrolling backgrounds and combat mechanics.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Sky Shark (NES) article
